4
INTRODUCTION TO FACEBOOK
Facebook has certainly gotten their share of bad press over the past few years. However
you feel about Mark Zuckerberg and his empire, one thing is for certain: Facebook
provides business owners a massive potential audience, and they do it for free.
To put it into perspective, if Facebook was a country, it would have the largest population
in the world. No matter what you sell, your customers are on Facebook (period).
That said, it is true that building an organic following on Facebook is not as easy as it
once was. The good news is that 99% of companies do Facebook completely wrong, so
you can succeed if you do it right. This guide will focus on helping you achieve the
following objectives:
While we will cover a wide range of specific features and strategies, there is one thing
that always matters above all else: putting the right message in front of the right person.
If you do, you will grow.
A snap shot of Grant’s typical monthly Facebook stats
The reason we win on Facebook is because we understand
the game. Remember, it’s their sandbox. Above all else,
put the user first, and Facebook will be happy.
DRIVE LEADS AND SALES
BUILD REAL RELATIONSHIPS
INCREASE OVERALL CREDIBILITY
SUPPORT YOUR CUSTOMERS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

FACEBOOK TERMINOLOGY
Here’s a quick-reference guide with some useful Facebook terms and phrases...
There is nothing more valuable than having an audience.
I used to travel a thousand miles to speak to a few hundred
people. Facebook gives you billions to work with.
This helps you manage your Page's Timeline like the list of posts and comments by your
Page. Only people who manage your Page can see the Activity Log.
ACTIVITY LOG
This is a group of people who can potentially see your Facebook posts. While this is
primarily a term used in advertising, realize that when done right, your organic (free)
Facebook activity and advertising will work together.
AUDIENCE
This shows the views of your video posts at each moment as a percentage of all views,
including videos shorter than 3 seconds.
AUDIENCE RETENTION
It is a marker that appears next to your name that shows people who you are or what
you've accomplished in a group. Group member’s also can earn a badge by participating.
BADGE
It is a post to your Page's timeline that you can apply money to in order to boost it to
an audience of your choice. It'll show up in your target audience's Facebook News
Feed as an ad.
BOOSTED POST
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 43 --

It is a free web-based Page that companies can build on Facebook to expand their
internet presence. It is the main component of your business efforts on Facebook.
BUSINESS PAGE
It is an automated messaging software that uses flows and automation to converse with
people. Bots are programmed to identify questions, provide answers, and execute tasks.
From a visitors' perspective, they're a friendly and accessible time-saver.
CHATBOT
It is any action a user takes on a Facebook Page or one of its posts. Examples are reactions
(including likes), comments and shares. It can also include saves, viewing a video or
clicking on a link.
ENGAGEMENT
It is a calendar-based feature of Facebook that can be used to notify users of upcoming
occasions. Events can be created by anyone, and can be public or private. The creator can
invite their friends, members of a group, or fans of a Page.
EVENT
These are the number of views your Facebook Page has gotten from website URLs that
aren't Facebook-owned properties.
EXTERNAL REFERRERS
People who liked or followed your Page.
FANS
Facebook admittedly isn’t my favorite platform these
days, but we are very active on; Think like a business
owner, not a consumer. Go where the money is.
6
When a user follows a Facebook profile or a Facebook Page, you will be able to see their
public posts on your Newsfeed.
FOLLOW

Certain features on Facebook get favored by their
algorithm, meaning they are more likely to get seen by
your audience. Live video is one of those features.
It is a Page created for an organization or business to promote activities. In a group, group
members can post their thoughts on the wall and interact through discussion threads.
GROUP
Hashtags turn words or phrases into clickable links when added to posts, Pages or groups.
It is very helpful to users to find posts about topics they're interested in.
HASHTAG
It is a post that contains an image. The optimal size for a Facebook post (shared)
image is 1,200 x 630 pixels.
IMAGE POST
Impressions are the number of times your Page post is displayed, whether the
post is clicked or not.
IMPRESSIONS
Facebook Insights provide information about your Page's performance. It shows which
posts have the most engagement and see who your audience are on Facebook.
INSIGHTS
This Facebook feature lets you broadcast a live video where your friends and followers can
watch and engage with your broadcast.
LIVE
7
This is a special type of Page post that lets you highlight important moments on your
Page's Timeline.
MILESTONE

People now get their “news” from social networks more
than they do newspapers. This creates an amazing
opportunity for small businesses to compete globally.
This is the total number of minutes your video was watched, including replays.
MINUTES VIEWED
Difference between the number of people who liked your Page and the number of
people who unliked your Page over a specific period of time.
NET LIKES
A user's newsfeed contains the posts of the status updates, posts, app activities, and Likes
from people, Pages, and Groups they associated with.
NEWSFEED
It is the number of people who saw your content for free without boosting it. This
includes users that are shown your posts on their own feed or because their friend
interacted with your post.
ORGANIC REACH
The five different roles you can assign people who help you manage your Page are:
admin, editor, moderator, advertiser, and analyst. They can login into their personal
Facebook accounts and manage your Page from there.
PAGE ROLES
The number of users who saw a specific post from your Page through a Facebook Ad or
Promoted (boosted) post.
PAID REACH
8
This shows the highest number of concurrent viewers who viewed your live broadcast for
at least three seconds. The curve you see in the graph shows the number of simultaneous
viewers watching different points of your live broadcast for at least three seconds.
PEAK LIVE VIEWERS

To succeed on social media, you’ve got to change your
entire mindset on sharing. It can feel awkward at first,
but the more you do it, the easier it becomes.
These are your posts, likes and comments on your Page's Timeline whether you're logged
into Facebook as yourself and not the Page. Under Page Settings > Post Attribution, you
can change the default to post as the individual rather than the Page. With this enabled,
when anyone who manages your Page creates a post or comments, it’ll be attributed to
that individual rather than the Page.
POST ATTRIBUTION
The number of users who have seen your Page's post. It counts as reaching someone
when it's shown on their Newsfeed.
POST REACH
It is the number of people who received impressions of a Page post. Reach might be less
than impressions since one user can make multiple impressions.
REACH
These are short, user-created photo or video collections that disappear after 24 hours. They
can be seen on the top of your Newsfeed along with the posted stories if you are friends.
STORIES
When people visit Pages with locations, they may see the option to suggest edits to the
Page. Suggested Edits are information that might be missing on the Page such as
category, phone number, or address. Page admins can confirm or remove information
that has been suggested.
SUGGESTED EDITS
When you pin a post, it will stay at the top of your timeline for 7 days. After that, it
returns to its proper position in your Timeline. It will be the first post users see when
they visit your Facebook Page.
PINNED POST

Tagging a user, Page, or place on your post associates your post to them. Your post can
be shown on their own Newsfeed and Timeline depending on their settings.
TAGGING
These are Facebook posts that only contain text.
TEXT POST
10
One of the stats I watch the most is my total Reach.
When it comes to making money, the more people who
know you, the more people will follow you!
This is where a visitor can see the posts of the user, Page, or Group.
TIMELINE
It's a Facebook account setting that lets you approve or reject posts that you've been
tagged in to appear on your Timeline.
TIMELINE REVIEW
It is the number of unique users who have actually seen any content related to your
Page. The sum of Post Reach will not be equal to Total Reach since Pages can reach
people through content other than posts. Example is, if a user visits a Page after
searching for it, they'll be counted in Total Reach but not on Post Reach.
TOTAL REACH
This is the number of distinct people who viewed your video at least once. We’ll show
you a breakdown of people who saw your video organically and through paid media
(like a boosted post).
UNIQUE VIEWERS
Facebook allows you to create a username that is unique to your page. When you do, a
vanity URL is automatically created. For example, facebook.com/yourusename.
VANITY URL

These are Pages and Profiles that are verified by Facebook to let the users know that
they're authentic. These are usually seen on Pages or Profiles for celebrities, public
figures, global brands, businesses, and media. The mark of a verified Profile or Page is a
blue badge next to the Page's name.
VERIFIED PAGE
11
This is calculated as the total watch time of your video divided by the total number of
video plays (including replays). If you see a point in your video with a significant drop-off,
look at your content to understand which parts of your video might not have been as
engaging to people. You can select a specific moment on the engagement graph to
watch the corresponding video clip.
VIDEO AVERAGE WATCH TIME
The Video Engagement graph shows you how aggregate audiences engaged at specific
points during a Live broadcast. This means you can see particular aspects throughout
your broadcast of when people were moved to react to, comment on, or share the video.
VIDEO ENGAGEMENT
It is a user-generated or shared post that contains a video.
VIDEO POST

Don’t overlook the value of your personal profile. While
profiles are limited to 5000 friends, your personal posts do
get viewed more. Use it as the catalyst for your Page.
12
FACEBOOK PAGE SETUP
The first step of getting your business on Facebook is to set up a Business Page. For sake
of clarity, it is important to distinguish a Business Page from a personal profile.
Every user on Facebook must have a personal profile. Your profile is used to login to
Facebook and communicate with friends. Your Facebook account allows you to do
everything from posting cat memes to raising money for charity.
PROFILE VS. BUSINESS PAGE
Custom Lists
Most people aren’t aware of this, but Facebook allows you
to create contact lists within your friends tab of your
Profile. Use this to create a list of people who would be
interested in your business topic, as well as those who
will be supportive of you in all you do. When you post and
target these groups specifically, you will have much
better engagement, and therefore are much more likely
for the post to be displayed to these users.
Business Page
Your business Page will become your public homepage on
Facebook. Pages provide most of the same functionality
as a profile, but they are specifically for your company.
Depending on your business type, this may be a personal
brand, a corporation or any type of organization. Different
types of Pages have slightly varying features and design,
but they are largely the same.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 43 --

We will discuss creating and customizing your Page in great detail, but it is important to
understand the relationship between your profile and Page. Once your Page is ready,
you will be able to participate on Facebook (post, comment, like, etc) using either your
profile OR your Page.
You can easily toggle back and forth between the two, and as we’ll discuss, there is a
time and place for each. For example, once you create your Page, it is recommended
that you invite your friends and family to follow your Page using your personal profile.
We will go into the specifics of setting your Page up, but first, let’s get clear on your
overall strategy. There are two very important questions you need to answer before
investing time and energy into Facebook.
Never forget that Facebook is a social community, so this isn’t going to be all about your
product or service. In fact, it is far less about you than it is those people that you serve.
The main reason for question 1 is so that you can show up every day and provide as
much value as possible.
13
Your Page will always rank high when someone searches for you
PAGE STRATEGY AND AUDIENCE
1. Who are you and what are you all about?
2. Who is your customer and what do they want?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 43 --

Remember that you can’t be all things to all people.
Before expanding online, be the best in the world for a
niche. I spent years teaching sales to auto dealerships.
Your first step in this process is to begin brainstorming your brand’s story. This could be
about your company, or your founder, or likely a combination of the two. It all depending
on your business. Regardless, it all starts by asking the right questions...

What have you accomplished?
What have you failed at?
What do you do every day?
What is your ultimate mission?
What makes you unique?
What has happened worth sharing?

After you’ve gotten some clarity around how you want to present your brand, it is time to
turn focus to defining your audience. Again, this starts with brainstorming. Try to think
outside of your particular product or industry. Here are some questions that will help...
Keep in mind, this is more than simply researching your competitors. For example, is
there a magazine that your audience would read? Follow their Page and like their
content. Is there a group that your target demographic would follow?
Join and participate.
Having this experience and knowledge will help you in all aspects of your Facebook
strategy. Pay attention to how other companies and experts position themselves.
This will assist in your Page design, but also in your ongoing content creation and
management.
Once you’ve started to map these things out, the next step is to immerse yourself in
Facebook as if you were a specific member of your desired audience…
What type of content does your audience consume? (print media, online, etc)
What influencers/companies does your audience follow?
What type of products does your audience purchase?
What type of groups (online and offline) does your audience participate in?
15

THEY WOULD FOLLOW

It can help you discover both relevant Pages and content ideas.

Now that you understand the importance of your Page and have identified your
audience, it is time to go to work. The good news is that Facebook provides a
step-by-step interface to help get your page created and launched
(10xresource.com/fb5).
BASIC PAGE DESIGN
SETUP AND CUSTOMIZATION
16
Profile Picture - When it makes sense, use a photo of a person. If you use a logo,
consider making it somehow eye catching.
Cover Photo - This banner should ideally include an image that portrays your company
visually. Your cover can also contain a slogan and/or some basic text to promote your
product or service. Note that your cover will display differently on desktop and mobile,
so be sure to click the icons to preview.
If you don’t have a designer try hiring one on Fiverr (10xresource.com/fiverr)
or using Canva to create a design yourself (10xresource.com/canva)

-- 16 of 43 --

After you’ve created your page, be sure to “View as” to
see what it looks like from a user’s perspective. It should
effectively convey the essence of your company in a glance.
Your Facebook page allows you to share information about your business. To edit the
info you display, click Edit Details then Update Your Information. Note that while all
fields are optional, Facebook generally encourages providing contact information.
EDITING DETAILS AND SETTINGS
Page Name - This will likely be your company name, or a simple name that describes
what you do. While this can be edited, you will want to consider this before choosing as it
can be a bit of a process to change.
Category - You can choose up to three categories for your page. When editing, start
typing various topics to get recommendations. These will help people find your business.
Description - Type a short and concise sentence that describes what you do in as few
words as possible, including how you benefit your customers. Users will often see this
description when they hover over your profile image.
Username - While not pictured above, after publishing your basic information you will
want to select a unique username for your page. Your username will become your URL
for your page. For example, facebook.com/usernamehere. You can edit this from the
Page Info section.
Phone number
Email address
Physical address
Hours of operation
Website links
Social links
Page Transparency
Facebook wants to make their platform safe for users. One of the things you
can do to help your page is to claim it in the Page Transparency section.
This is a simple verification process and can be found in Edit Details.
Recommended Info:

18
Before you publish anything, make sure to preview what
it will look like on a mobile device. The vast majority of
people on Facebook are using their phone these days.
As you have likely seen, Facebook allows you to provide a wide range of content and
functionality on your page. This is done with what they call tabs (menu at top) and
sections (content on the page). You can control which content appears and in what
order, which is found in the Settings area.
Here are the general options, which can vary slightly depending on page type:
Here are details on a few notable sections:
CUSTOMIZE TABS AND SECTIONS
Shop - This is primarily for ecommerce companies. If you have a store on Shopify for
example, you can easily integrate it to have your products appear on Facebook.
Offers - This is great for providing specials such as online coupon codes or local
promotions.
Events - If you are hosting events, either online or offline, you can use Facebook events
to help promote, get people to RSVP and keep attendees updated.
To remove a section, simply toggle the switch to off. To rearrange sections, simply click
and hold on the dot icon on the left and move up and down.
Home
Services
Reviews
Shop
Offers
Photos
Videos
Events
About
Community
Jobs
Groups
Live
Guides
NOTE: The navigation on Facebook pages is constantly changing, but if you want to
access something not found in the main menu, it is likely in Settings. If it is not in
Settings, you can usually find it by clicking Manage or the three dots.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 43 --

Always ask your customers to recommend you on
Facebook. People do research before buying, and having
strong reviews is one of the best thing you can do for sales.
REVIEWS AND SUPPORT
One of the most important aspects of your business on Facebook is reviews.
Facebook uses a simple recommendation system that simply allows people to say
they either recommend you, or don’t recommend you. Keep an eye on your reviews
and always reply to them, whether good or bad.
Realize that while Facebook can certainly be a valuable marketing tool for your
business, it is also a channel for customer support (whether you like it or not). Your
customers will find you on Facebook, and they will expect you to be actively replying
to their inquiries.
Facebook highly encourages businesses to reply to all messages, and to do so quickly.
In fact, your response rate and speed are displayed on your page.
19

Your reviews on Facebook even show up on Google
Facebook has separated their messaging capability into an integrated app
called Messenger. While you can manage your businesses messages from
within your Facebook page Manager, you can also use third party tools,
which we will discuss a bit later.

-- 19 of 43 --

20
POSTING AND CONTENT
With your Business Page ready to go, you’re ready to start focusing on your content.
When you post to Facebook, it will be displayed on your Page (in what is referred to as
your Timeline) and it can also appear in the News Feed of your Page followers.
Facebook allows for a wide range of post types, we will cover the most common and
important ones here...
TYPES OF FACEBOOK POSTS
Update (Text Post)
While it is recommended that you use an image or video when possible, you can quickly
and easily share a thought with text only.
Photos/Images
The most common type of posts, these can range from photos to art, and from
inspiration to humor. Images can be of varying dimensions, but we generally design to
be square.
Videos
You can easily upload videos as a post. After they’ve been added, you can also create
Playlists on your page.
Sample of random image post styles from a few days of posting
Don’t feel like your posts need to be all polished or
professional. I like to let people into my world using my
smartphone. Your phone is good enough to do everything.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 43 --

21
Live Video
Probably the most engaging posting format, Live allows you to stream in real time, and
interact with your audience while you do it. This feature can be used for interviews,
product tutorials, conferences, or just to share an impromptu message with your
audience.
Stories
Stories are a collection of images and videos which are played for users sequentially
back-to-back. Stories are unique in that they disappear in 24 hours, but they can get a lot
of views during that period.
GIFs
These are the fun animated images you see online. Facebook has a large library of GIFs
for you to choose from.
Note that we provide a wealth of content ideas in an upcoming chapter. We’ve also
provided a checklist to help ensure you optimize each of your posts
(10xdownload.com/fb4).
When adding a post update you may want to use the following options...
There are many more options that come and go, but these are the most important.
Additional Posting Features
Tagging
Attach any user or Page by typing @ then the name
Check-in
Allows you to tie your post to a geographic location
Feeling/Activity
Label your post with how/what you are doing
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 43 --

People ask me how often they should post. My question
back is always “how often do you want to make money?”
You will never breakthrough without persistence.
22
While you should certainly post as you feel inspired, if you want to create a content
machine, you need to consistently plan.
Your first decision is to decide how often you want to post. This could range from a few
times a week to multple times per day. At 10X, we post on average 6 times per day.
Needless to say, it has worked out pretty well.
In addition to how often you post, you also need to plan out your desired content mix.
Your mix will include various content types (videos, images, lives, etc) the substance of
the posts (motivation, education, entertainment, etc) and finally your monetization
strategy (send to website, messenger, etc). All of this is laid out in the content planner
checklist (10xdownload.com/fb3).
We recommend planning out your content either weekly or monthly, whichever works
best for you. The important thing is that you are not only consistent, but also remain
flexible. Don’t be afraid to post outside of your planned schedule, should an opportunity
arise. Those are usually the best things to shares!
PLANNING AND MANAGEMENT
Last 30 days
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 43 --

If this all sounds like a lot, remember that it all comes
down to creating systems. While it will take a bit to get
used to, once you get in a groove, it is really quite simple.
There are 3 different ways you can post on Facebook…
METHODS FOR POSTING
Post in Facebook - This is simply using the standard post feature, which is the most
common way of doing it. The biggest downfall is you are unable to schedule posts in
advance.
Creator Studio - This is Facebook’s more robust content management area which is
available to all business owners. It allows for some more advanced features including
scheduling and editing.
3rd Party Management Platform - Many companies, including ours, utilize more
robust online software to handle the managment of posting and content.
23
In additon to managing your posts, there are a lot of other ongoing tasks involved with
managing your social. These include…
We’ve provided more detail on many of these items in later chapters. You can also find
an extended list in the Facebook Management checklist (10xdownload.com/fb5)
We use Sprout Social as we’ve found it to be a great tool to manage all of our
social platforms. It is especially helpful for managing team collaboration,
creating automated posting calendars, and producing multi-channel statistics.
Replying to Post Comments
Checking the Messenger inbox
Checking profile notifications
Checking page notifications
Reacting (like, heart, etc)
Commenting on other pages
Managing boosts in Ad Center
Participate in relevant groups
Tag and message influencers
Check page support inbox
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 43 --

Once you’ve wrapped your head around what it takes to manage your content and Page,
it’s time to get to work creating the content. It’s a never ending process, but it can be
quite fun once you get into it.
Video is one of these things that tends to scare people. Remember that nobody expects
you to be perfect, and you will only get better with time. If you look at Grant’s content ten
years ago, you’d be shocked to see how basic and low budget the production is. He just
focused on the actual substance, and kept showing up over and over again.
Here are some random video concepts to get you going...
Keep in mind you can create many styles of videos. They don’t have to involve you or a
company representative on camera. They can use stock video footage with voice-over,
simple animations or even animated text. The options are limitless. Regardless, you’ll
want to test a wide range of topics, especially until you find what best resonates with
your audience.
24
CONTENT CREATION IDEAS
Remember that this is all about making connection. You’ve
got to be willing to share and slowly reveal your brand
story. It’s like peeling back an onion one layer at a time.
CREATING VIDEO POSTS
The Weird Way to ___________
7 Simple Steps to Achieve ___________
Celebrity Secrets for ___________
The ___________ that Changed My Life
Why Most People ___________
3 Life Hacks for ___________
The 1 ___________ to Avoid At All Costs
The Worst ___________ of All Time
What Your ___________ Isn’t Telling You
Top 10 ___________
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 43 --

While there is no one formula for creating great videos, one
thing is for certain: they need to grab people’s attention fast.
You have three seconds to get someone to stop scrolling.
If you don’t know how to edit video, and you don’t have a team member
who does, then you’ll likely want to get an online editing software. Promo
makes it easy to make videos, and even provides video clips you can use at
no extra cost. (10xresource.com/promo)
CREATING IMAGE POSTS
Famous Quotes
Which Doesn’t Belong?
“Throwback” Photo
Family/Pets/Travel
Epic Fails
Love it or Hate it?
Fill in the Blank
Message Screenshot
Customer Success
Inspirational Share
Before and After
Lifestyle Photos
Recommended Books
Quick Life Tips
25
Videos can vary in length as well as dimension. Keep in mind that Facebook is also linked
to Instagram, which generally has taller videos. For this reason, we will sometimes make
our videos be square, allowing us to better optimize both channels.
Image based posts are often easier for new Facebook marketers in that they don't
require much advanced planning, on-camera talent, or post-production editing.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 43 --

26
When you see that my posts have thousands of comments,
realize it is only because so many of them are haters.
Haters fuel conversation. They also make me millions.
If you do not have a designer on staff, then consider using an online image
editor. Software services such as Canva are specifically designed for social
networks like Facebook. They are easy to use and automatically size your
images for you. (10xresource.com/canva)
Be sure to make your posts eye-catching as they have to get people to notice while
scrolling. Beyond the design, do whatever you can to make the post something people
want to engage with. This is achieved by writing post descriptions that speak directly to
the user, often asking for their thoughts or opinion.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 43 --

POSTING OPTIMIZATION TIPS
Success on Facebook is about more than the quantity and quality of your posts. You’ve
also got to make sure your content gets seen. The easiest way to help ensure that
happens is by posting at times when people are most active. Here is a chart created by
Sprout Social that shows the best times to post…
Regarding the general content of your posts, you’ve got to remember that on Facebook
you are not only creating content for your audience, but it helps to do it in a way that
Facebook will favor. We will talk more about this in the next chapter (specifically the
Facebook algorithm) but generally speaking, here are some rules that will help you
appease both users and the network...
27
Mix sales in with quality content
Share 80% value, 20% sales
Encourage people to like or comment
Share occasional personal posts
Talk about overcoming difficulties
Try to make others look good
Do...
Don’t be too salesly or pushy
Sell more than you provide value
Incentivise people to comment or like
Overshare constant personal posts
Complain about how hard things are
Try to make others look bad
Don’t...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 43 --

POST IMPROVEMENT STRATEGIES
You may have noticed in an earlier example (middle post on page 20) that Grant did a
very direct sales post asking people to invest $500,000. This breaks every “best practice”
imaginable. The result was very low engagement and reach compared to his normal
posts. That said, it also resulted in large sums of money transacting. It’s all about picking
and choosing the right time to go in for the sale.
28
Here are some other tips to help get your content seen by more eyeballs…
Tagging - While this should be done sparingly and appropriately, when you tag
another user’s account or another company’s page, they are likely to see the post.
If they engage with the post by liking, sharing or commenting, your post has a chance
to also reach their network.
Hashtags - If you’ve seen these things #, you’ve seen hashtags. By adding a word (or a
phrase connected as one word) behind a #, it will automatically add your post as a
search result for that term. That hashtag term will also become a link to the search
results for that term. So for example, if you put #10X in a post, when people search 10X
or click on a #10X link, they could find you.
Link Strategy - Generally speaking, Facebook does not like people leaving their
network. Whenever you post a link in a post, you run the risk of it reducing your reach.
Instead, try adding a link in a comment directly under your post.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 43 --

The #1 rule is that you can’t outsmart the Algorithm. Any
tricks will result in a short-term gain. Instead, learn what
the system wants, and play their game 80% of the time.
INSIGHTS AND THE ALGORITHM
29
GROWTH AND MONETIZATION
This section is all about knowing what numbers matter, how to find them, and
ultimately, how to grow them. In that spirit, the first thing you need to understand to
succeed on Facebook is that you are serving more than just people.
Imagine that you are playing on the biggest digital playground in the world. This
playground is filled with billions of individuals, all there for their own reasons. Some to
play, some to sell their candy, some simply because they do what they are told. While
your goal is to find the ones who want what you have, there is one other factor that you
need to take into consideration: the Algorithm.
The Algorithm runs the show. Nothing happens without the Algorithm’s approval. The
Algorithm decides what posts get approved, and who sees the posts in their newsfeed.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 43 --

PAGE RECOMMENDATIONS
Ultimately, Facebook is programming their algorithm to achieve the goal: provide the
best user experience possible. While Facebook certainly wants to make money (and they
make over $100 billion a year), they know that if they lose the users, they lose the money.
Therefore, anything that drives a positive user experience will get you more visibility.
In addition to getting your posts viewed by as many people as possible, an additional
benefit of getting favored by the algorithm is having your page recommended. This can
become a source of free traffic and leads, even for small pages. For example, here are the
pages that got recommended when we pulled up Success Magazine’s page…
We will discuss more of the strategies it takes to associate yourself with successful pages
in a later section. For now, realize that you’ve got to do things right, for a prolonged
period of time to get yourself in position for rapid growth.
30
These small pages got attached to Success because they played the game
One of the many potential violations on Facebook
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 43 --

31
As you grow, realize that you will likely encounter times where Facebook will penalize
you. Some of them you will be told about (such as Messenger restrictions) and others you
won’t (such as your reach suddenly decreasing). When you hit road bumps, realize that it
is part of the game. One of the easiest things to combat this is to avoid saying those
things that negatively trigger the algorithm.
Note that it is impossible to avoid all these words. Just make sure to use them sparingly
and when you use them, make it count. There are countless other ways to phrase things.
For example, you can say “show some love” or “zero out of pocket”.
While you shouldn’t stress occassional ups and downs that occur in your statistics, you
should keep an eye on them. The easiest way to do this is by using their Audience
Insights feature, which can be accessed in a few ways. Here is an example of some basics
stats, but you can drill deeper as desired…
PHRASES TO AVOID
ACTIVITY:
• Like
• Comment
• Share
• Tag
INCENTIVE:
• Free
• Win
• Contest
• Enter
PROMOTION:
• Ad
• Promotion
• Affiliate
• Sponsored
SELLING:
• Buy
• Discount
• Coupon
• Sale
The secret is to not care too much about the outcome of
any individual post. Some will work, and some won’t.
Learn from them all, but always play the numbers game.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 43 --

32
If your stats are not exciting at first, try not to let it get you down. This is a long game,
and even Grant took years to truly take off. Remember that you don’t need a million
fans, you just need a few thousand who truly love you for who you are.
While there will hopefully be a day where you will be able to post like Grant and have
thousands of people immediately see your posts, it won’t happen overnight. In order to
get there, it is going to a need strategy and a lot of ongoing interaction, both on your
page as well as on other areas of Facebook.
One of the things you need to decide is who you want to interact as on Facebook: your
personal profile or your page (business) profile...
FOLLOWERS AND ENGAGMENT
The social media war is won in the trenches: one post at
a time, one comment at a time, one like at a time. You’re
not done when you post, you’re just getting started.
Last 28 Days
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 43 --

33
Generally speaking, people would rather interact with a person than a logo, so consider
using your personal profile rather than you page. That said, if you have a brand built
around an individual (and your business profile picture is a photo of a person), then it
might make sense to interact as your page.
The important thing to remember that the same general rules apply to Facebook
etiquette as the real world. Treat people the way you want to be treated, and try not to
take things personally. It is inevitable that you will take criticism, which is a sign that
you’re starting to make progress.
Don’t be quick to delete negative comments, because they
are what gets the conversation started. I’ve found it is
often best to fuel the flames. Encourage the haters to hate.
Easily switch profiles between personal and page
TYPES OF INTERACTIONS
• Comment/Reply
• React (Like, Love, etc)
• Friend Request
• Direct Message
WHERE TO INTERACT
• Your Posts
• Other Page Posts
• Groups
• Messenger
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 43 --

34
BOOSTING AND AUDIENCES
Use these to get the conversation started…
As you create more and more content, you’ll have a certain percentage of them that
resonate with your audience. When you get a winner, you’ll want to spend some
money “boosting” that post to try to reach people who you believe would be good to
follow your page.
OPENING QUESTIONS
While your tendency might be to boost the posts that
don’t do well, it is actually quite the opposite. In business,
always put resources into what works, not what doesn’t.
Comment on worthy comments
Like or react to most comments
Tag with tact and purpose
Add value to competitor pages
Make strategic friend requests
Do...
Comment on every comment
Like or react to every comment
Tag frequently without strategy
Put links on competitor pages
Make random friend requests
Don’t...
• __________, love it or hate it?
• Which is better, __________ or __________?
• What would you do if you had __________?
• Worst/best __________ of all time?
• What you wish you knew when __________?
• Last time you __________?
• What advice can you give on __________?
• What GIF best describes __________?
• Describe __________ in 3 words?
• What makes you truly __________?
• What are you doing for __________?
• Someone who __________? Tag them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 43 --

35
BASICS OF BOOSTING
Boosting is basically turning your posts into a paid ad. While Facebook does provide an
advanced Ad Manager, it can be a bit daunting for beginners. While there are
advantages to using the Ad Manager, if you want to keep it simple, you can use the
Boost functionality without ever leaving Facebook.
When you click the button, you’ll find that it is step-by-step, and Facebook even
provides “learn more” links when needed.
The first thing you’ll do is to decide on your goal, which is the objective of your ad. You
will want to decide this, rather than letting Facebook decide automatically. Here are
your options and when to use each…
This button will appear for you on all posts
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 43 --

36
UNDERSTANDING AUDIENCES
The other important concept to understand is selecting your audience. There are a
number of options for choosing who your ad is displayed to...
Note that depending on which goal you choose, your ad will be given a different button,
and you’ll also have unique settings to complete.
Messages
People will get sent to your Messenger or Whatsapp, which is great if you
want to sell them services or start a conversation. Also good if you have a
Messenger bot, which we will discuss in the next section.
Engagement
This will find people who will like and comment on your posts. This is good
if your goal is to get more followers on your Facebook Page, but obviously
not designed for sales.
Website Visitors
This will allow to send people to any web page or funnel.
Calls
If you want to use a click-to-call option to get the phone ringing.
Targeting - This allows you to choose your
audience based on gender, age, location,
and a wide range of detailed targeting
factors. These include interest, behaviors
and advanced demographics. To see what
is available, you can start typing, or click
browse to see all categories.
Example targeting men who like
Polka music and the Dolphins
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 43 --

37
Organic social media and advertising now go hand-in-hand.
You don’t need to spend a lot, but if you’re just starting out
and you want to get found, it is pay-to-play.
BUDGETING AND PLACEMENT
It is usually a good idea to start your budgeting low, and grow it as you see favorable
results. Depending on your goal, you can start to see significant engagement for as little
as $5 a day. If your goal is to make sales, you will need to spend more. In that case, it is
recommended that you learn how to use the Facebook Ad Manager.
Regarding your ad placement, you should post wherever you want to be found. If you
have a strong Instagram account, or you want to, then you should post there. If you
don’t, then it is fine to skip it.
People who like your page - This is good if you just want your followers to see the post.
People who like your page and their friends - Recommended if you want to expand
your network a bit.
People in your area - For local business or local events.
Custom Audiences - This is a more advanced feature that is generally comprised of
people who visit your website. To utilize this, you must install your Facebook pixel
on your site.
While it may sound intimidating, one thing we highly
recommend doing immediately is installing your ad pixel on all
your websites and funnels. This is a small tracking code that will
allow you to create audiences from those people who land on
your pages. This is an extremely valuable audience.
The first step is to create a Facebook business account, which you can do at
business.facebook.com.
After doing so, you may follow steps and install the pixel yourself, should you wish
(10xresource.com/fba18). Or to make things easy, we recommend hiring an
inexpensive professional to do it from Fiverr (10xresource.com/fiverr)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 43 --

38
REACTION FOLLOW INVITES
TIPS, TRICKS AND HACKS
This is a very simple technique to help you grow your followers. Most people don’t
realize this, but when you click on the number of reactions you have on a post (likes,
loves, etc) it opens up a screen that let’s you invite anyone who reacted who is not
already following you.
Note that you are sometimes limited in how many people you can invite in one day,
but you’ll be fine when you get started. This method is especially valuable when
you boost your posts with the goal of engagement, because you’ll get a lot of
people who like!
Click on the number
of reactions...
Then invite people to follow your page!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 43 --

39
This is another easy-to-use feature, but even less people know about this one! It can be
used for multiple purposes…
As you know, one of the challenges with Facebook is getting your posts seen by the
people who follow you. To empower users to have more control over what they see,
Facebook created a feature that allows anyone to choose their favorite pages. This allows
posts by that page to appear at the top of the news feed.
This is perfect for those fans who truly want to see your posts. You can teach people how
to do this by giving instructions via email, sharing it with customers, encouraging people
on Facebook lives, or any other way you can think of. People love learning this “trick” and
it helps you because it will give you more engagement on every post you make!
FAVORITE FOLLOW FEATURE
Click the 3 dots, then Follow Settings... 	Choose Favorite from the menu to complete!
To help you closely follow pages in your industry.
To allow your followers to see more of your posts.
Start by liking the page then...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 43 --

INFLUENCER INNER CIRCLE METHOD
40
This is a more advanced strategy. It will take some time to implement, but can pay
long-term dividends once successful. Before beginning, it helps to take a second to fully
comprehend why you want to do it, and why it works…
YOUR OUTCOMES:
To create real connections with leading influencers and companies in your
desired industry (i.e. the “inner circle”)
To have Faceboook associate your page with their page, making your page
get recommended to your ideal audience
Start by connecting with fans of other pages, allowing you to get
associations and similar friends, increasing credibility
Gradually work your way further into the inner center by strategically liking,
commenting, making friend requests, etc.
THE STRATEGY:
Top Fans - These are individuals on industry
pages who have this by comments
Minor Influencers - These are pages or group
owners with some following and activity.
Major Incfluencers - These are the biggest
page and group owners in your niche.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 43 --

41
Slowly befriend Top Fans of other pages in your industry (just a few per day).
At the same time, actively comment and like posts by minor and major influencers.
Do occassional posts inspired by minor influencers and tag them in comments.
Once you’ve established some connection, attempt to befriend minor influencers.
Next, do occassional posts inspired by major influencers and tag in comments.
Once you’ve established some connection, attempt to befriend major influencers.
STEPS INVOLVED IN THE PROCESS
1
2
3
4
5
6
NOTE: There is no exact formula for this method. As long as you understand the
concept and do it naturally, you can slowly start working your way toward the inner
circle. The more connections you make, the easier it gets, and the more Facebook will
favor your page in results for industry terms, etc.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
